title: Homemade Cheeseburgers & Oven Fries
description: A classic family meal made easy at home! Juicy cheeseburgers paired with crispy oven-baked fries make for a delicious and kid-approved dinner.
instructions: Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C). Line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
Wash and cut potatoes into "fry" shapes. In a large bowl, toss fries with olive oil, salt, pepper, garlic powder, and onion powder until evenly coated.
Spread fries in a single layer on the prepared baking sheet. Bake for 25-30 minutes, flipping halfway, until golden brown and crispy.
While fries are baking, gently form ground beef into four patties, about 1/2 inch thick. Season both sides with salt and pepper.
Heat a large skillet or grill pan over medium-high heat. Cook patties for 3-4 minutes per side, or until desired doneness.
During the last minute of cooking, place a cheese slice on each patty to melt.
Lightly toast hamburger buns. Assemble burgers with patties, cheese, and your favorite toppings like lettuce, tomato, ketchup, and mustard.
Serve cheeseburgers immediately with hot oven fries.
tips: For extra crispy fries, soak the cut potatoes in cold water for 30 minutes before tossing with oil and seasoning. Pat them very dry before baking.
Don't overwork the ground beef when forming patties; this can make them tough. Handle them gently.
Customize your burgers with different cheeses, sauces, or additional toppings like pickles or onions.
